# New Epics To Implement

## Update our prompts for Rate Limiting

I keep getting rate limited by the GitHub CoPilot when I try to use it to generate code for this project. We need to update my prompts to include instructions that will force the AI to work slower. For example, it should not call the CoPilot API more often than once every 2 minutes if that is something we can control.

I'm open to recommendations and I realize this is a challenging problem but it is more important that our prompts work until they are done over working quickly.

If we can't do anything else, we can insert wait statements into our prompts to force the AI to wait before it continues.

## Tests should have 100% branch coverage

We should have 100% branch coverage for all of our unit tests. This means that we should have tests that cover all possible branches of our code, including all if statements, switch statements, and any other branching logic. We should use the vitest code coverage tool to measure our branch coverage and ensure that we are meeting this requirement.

We also need to update our script in our package.json file so that it always runs the code coverage check when it runs the tests. This will ensure that our quality validation check includes the branch coverage requirement and that we are always measuring our branch coverage when we run our tests.

## You added ignore paths to .jscpd.json that should not be there

Remove these ignore paths from the .jscpd.json file:
"**/global/global-screener/**",
"**/global/global-universe/**",
"**/global/global-summary.ts",
"**/global/global-summary.html",
"**/accounts/account-summary/account-summary.ts",
"**/accounts/account-summary/account-summary.html",
"**/routes/trades/get-closed-trades/**",
"**/routes/trades/get-open-trades/**"

Fix the code.

Also add instructions to our Architecture document using the bmad-architect skill to ensure that we do not alter any of our quality check tools unless explicitly told to do so. This include jscpd, eslint, prettier and any other tools we use for quality checks. We should not alter the configuration of these tools unless we have a very good reason to do so and it has been approved by the user via the prompt skill. We should always document any changes we make to these tools in our Architecture document.

## Sort by multiple columns on the Universe screen

Currently, we can only sort by one column at a time on the Universe screen. We need to be able to sort by at least 2 columns on this screen. For example, we should be able to sort by "Position" and then by "Symbol" within the same sort. This is a common feature in tables and it would be very useful for our users. Please implement multi-column sorting on the Universe screen. Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that multi-column sorting is working correctly on the Universe screen.

## Current State of Sort/Filter in UI on Refresh

We currently save the sort and filter state, and, I assume (it seems to be working), we restore the sort and filter state on refresh. However, the UI does not reflect the restored state in either the filters or in the sorts (where it displays the sort direction in the column header). This is a problem because it is confusing to the user. They may not realize that the sort and filter state is being restored and they may think that the sort and filter state is not being restored. Please fix this so that the UI reflects the restored sort and filter state on refresh. Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that the UI reflects the restored sort and filter state on refresh.

## Storybook Should Display Light/Dark Themes

Storybook should allow us to display the components in both light and dark themes and the snapshot testing should take an image of each.

## No checkbox columns in the Screener screen

We no longer need the checkbox columns in the Screener screen.

- Remove the checkbox columns from the Screener screen.
- Remove the related code on the backend. ie, the columns in the database, any sorting code related to the checkbox columns, and any code related to updating the checkbox columns.
- Update the code for "Update Universe" on the universe screen and backend so that it no longer checks that all checkboxes have been checked before adding the store to the universe. It should just add any symbols in the screener results to the universe without checking any checkboxes.
- Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that the checkbox columns have been removed from the Screener screen and that the "Update Universe" functionality still works correctly without checking any checkboxes.

## Storybook BaseTable Component

Currently there is not much to see for the BaseTable component because it is empty. Add a basic default story and a few variations based on how we us it in the app so we see something that we can visually verify in Storybook and use for snapshot testing.

You may have to mock data to get the display to show something in the table. You can look at the code for the tables in the app to see what data they are using and how they are using the BaseTable component to get an idea of how to set up the stories for the BaseTable component. Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that the stories for the BaseTable component are displaying correctly in Storybook and that the snapshot testing is working correctly for the BaseTable component.

## Janky Scrolling on the Tables

This is hard to describe and I'm not sure how you'll be able to see this so I'm saving this for last.

When you scroll down on the tables, at some point, the scrolling starts jumping up and down as you scroll.

Make sure that the row height on all the tables is the same height so that when we compute the 10 row buffer for the virtual scrolling, it is using the correct height for the rows. I would prefer to have all the rows on all the tables be the same height. If that is not possible, we will need to set the row height for each table explicitly.

If this is not the problem, I'll need you to investigate this issue further and find out what is causing the janky scrolling on the tables. Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that the scrolling on the tables is smooth and does not jump up and down as you scroll.
